Container.Name: InvoiceShelf
Container.Overview:
This is the template of InvoiceShelf that just works.
InvoiceShelf is a self-hosted, open source invoicing and estimates app built with Laravel and VueJS. This template runs the webapp only (pgsql variant) and expects an external PostgreSQL 16 server/container to already be running - it does NOT deploy its own database. Before starting this container, create a database and a dedicated user for InvoiceShelf on your existing Postgres instance, then fill in the DB_* variables below. Reference compose file: https://github.com/InvoiceShelf/docker/blob/master/docker-compose.pgsql.yml
Storage is handled with Docker-managed named volumes (invoiceshelf_storage, invoiceshelf_modules) so the container works immediately with zero manual setup - no directories to create, no permissions to fix. Docker auto-populates them with the correct files and ownership from the image on first start.
Note: this means logo uploads, custom PDF templates, and logs live inside Docker's own volume storage rather than a visible /mnt/user/appdata folder, so they are NOT included in typical Appdata Backup plugin runs. Your invoices, customers, and payments are unaffected either way since those live in your external Postgres database. If you want those files backed up too, see the "Backing up named volumes" note in Support/docs, or switch these to bind-mounted paths if you prefer visibility over zero-touch setup.
If running behind Nginx reverse proxy with a nonstandard port (eg. https://your-domain.com:50443), the custom configuration for location '/' must be: proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Host $host:50443; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Port 50443;
Container.Requires: Requires an already-running external PostgreSQL 16 server with a database + user created for InvoiceShelf. This template does not create the database for you.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Host port for the InvoiceShelf web UI. If you're routing to this container by name through a reverse proxy on a custom network, you can leave this or remove it.

Config.Name: APP_NAME
Config.Description: Application name

Config.Name: APP_URL
Config.Description: Primary public URL used for generated links (emails, PDFs). Use your external https URL if you access from outside; local network access still works independently as long as SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS below includes it too.

Config.Name: SESSION_DOMAIN
Config.Description: Leave BLANK if you access InvoiceShelf from more than one hostname/IP (e.g. local IP + external domain) - a blank value lets the session cookie scope itself to whichever host was used. Only set this to a single domain if you exclusively access via that one domain.

Config.Name: SANCTUM_STATEFUL_DOMAINS
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of every host:port you'll load the app from - e.g. your LAN IP:port AND your external reverse-proxy domain:port. Include the port whenever it isn't the default 80/443.

Config.Name: TRUSTED_PROXIES
Config.Description: Required for correct HTTPS/host detection behind a reverse proxy. Set to * to trust all proxies, or restrict to your reverse proxy's container/LAN IP for tighter security.

Config.Name: DB_HOST
Config.Description: Hostname/container name or IP of your existing PostgreSQL 16 server. If it's on the serverproxy network, its container name works; otherwise use the Unraid server IP.

Config.Name: DB_PORT
Config.Description: PostgreSQL port

Config.Name: DB_DATABASE
Config.Description: Database name (must already exist on the external Postgres server)

Config.Name: DB_USERNAME
Config.Description: Database user (must already exist and own the database)

Config.Name: DB_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Database user password

Config.Name: APP_ENV
Config.Description: Application environment

Config.Name: APP_DEBUG
Config.Description: Enable debug mode

Config.Name: DB_CONNECTION
Config.Description: Database driver

Config.Name: DB_PASSWORD_FILE
Config.Description: Optional: path to a Docker secret file containing the DB password, instead of DB_PASSWORD

Config.Name: CACHE_STORE
Config.Description: Cache driver

Config.Name: SESSION_DRIVER
Config.Description: Session driver

Config.Name: SESSION_LIFETIME
Config.Description: Session lifetime in minutes

Config.Name: AUTORUN_ENABLED
Config.Description: Run startup automation on container boot

Config.Name: AUTORUN_LARAVEL_MIGRATION
Config.Description: Auto-run DB migrations on boot (set true for first run against a fresh DB, then false)

Config.Name: AUTORUN_LARAVEL_OPTIMIZE
Config.Description: Auto-run Laravel optimize on boot

Config.Name: PHP_OPCACHE_ENABLE
Config.Description: Enable PHP OPcache
